title: Japan mein Truck Driver ki salary kitni hoti hai?
excerpt: Curious about the earning potential for Indian truck drivers in Japan under the SSW visa? We break down the base pay, overtime, and living costs.
content:
Japan is facing a massive shortage of logistics and transport workers. For Indian drivers with an HMV license, the SSW (Specified Skilled Worker) visa opens up an incredible opportunity to work in Japan.

Average Salary Breakdown

The starting salary for an SSW truck driver in Japan typically ranges from ¥220,000 to ¥280,000 per month (approx. ₹1.2 Lakh to ₹1.5 Lakh). However, this is just the base pay.

With overtime and bonuses, many drivers take home well over ¥300,000. Companies often provide subsidized housing, bringing your living costs down significantly.

Requirements

  • Pass the JLPT N4 or JFT-Basic language test.
  • Pass the Automobile Transportation SSW Skill Test.
  • Hold a valid Indian Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) license.
author: NihonNest Team

title: SSW Visa ke liye kya documents chahiye — Complete Checklist
excerpt: Don't let missing paperwork delay your Japan dream. Here is the ultimate document checklist you need before applying for the SSW visa.
content:
Applying for the SSW visa can feel overwhelming, but organizing your documents early makes the process smooth.

1. Basic Identity Documents

  • Valid Passport (at least 6 months validity)
  • Recent Passport Size Photographs (35mm x 45mm, white background)
  • Aadhaar Card & PAN Card

2. Education & Skill Proof

  • 10th and 12th Marksheets
  • JLPT N4 or JFT-Basic Passing Certificate
  • SSW Sector-Specific Skill Test Certificate

3. Employment Documents (Once Selected)

  • Job Offer Letter from the Japanese Company
  • Employment Contract
  • Certificate of Eligibility (CoE) — issued by Japanese Immigration
author: Visa Expert

title: JLPT N4 6 mahine mein kaise pass karein
excerpt: Passing JLPT N4 is the biggest hurdle for most candidates. Follow our proven 6-month study plan to clear the exam on your first attempt.
content:
The JLPT N4 or JFT-Basic is the absolute minimum language requirement for the SSW visa. Here is how you can clear it in 6 months from zero.

Months 1-2: Master the Basics

Focus entirely on Hiragana and Katakana. Do not use Romaji (English letters). Start learning basic grammar structures (particles wa, ga, o, ni) and daily greetings.

Months 3-4: Vocabulary & Kanji

You need to know about 300 Kanji and 1,500 vocabulary words for N4. Use flashcard apps like Anki or Quizlet. Learn 10 new words and 3 new Kanji every day without fail.

Months 5-6: Mock Tests & Listening

Listening (Choukai) is where most Indian students fail. Watch Japanese dramas, listen to Japanese podcasts, and do at least 10 full mock tests under timed conditions.
author: Language Sensei

title: Japan mein Indian khana kahan milta hai?
excerpt: Missing home? Discover how easy it is to find dal, roti, and Indian spices in Japanese supermarkets and specialty stores.
content:
One of the biggest worries for Indians moving to Japan is the food. Will you survive if you are purely vegetarian? Absolutely.

Indian Groceries are Everywhere

You no longer have to bring a suitcase full of spices. Halal shops and Indian grocery stores are scattered across Tokyo, Osaka, and Nagoya. Websites like Ambika Veg and Baticrom deliver fresh Indian vegetables, atta, paneer, and spices right to your door.

Japanese Supermarkets (Super)

Local supermarkets sell incredibly fresh vegetables, rice, lentils, and dairy. You can easily cook dal-chawal or sabzi every day. Tofu is a great, cheap alternative to paneer!
author: NihonNest Team

title: SSW Automobile Transportation Skills Test — Complete Guide
excerpt: Everything you need to know about the newly introduced transport skill test for truck, taxi, and bus drivers in Japan.
content:
In 2024, Japan added the Automobile Transportation sector to the SSW visa. This is a game-changer for Indian drivers.

What is the Skill Test?

The test evaluates your knowledge of Japanese traffic rules, vehicle maintenance, customer service (for taxi/bus), and safe driving practices. The test is conducted in Japanese, so N4 level comprehension is required.

Test Format

It is a computer-based test (CBT) consisting of multiple-choice questions. There is no actual driving component in this initial skill test, as your practical skills are verified by your Indian driving license.

How to Prepare

The Japanese government provides official textbooks and study materials for free on their ministry website. Memorize the traffic signs and basic safety inspection protocols.
author: Transport Sector Expert
